source_of_truth: draft.md contains manuscript prose; this registry is an audit and navigation layer.

objects.description: Simultaneous impact-only observed residual model u_t = B0 epsilon_t + eta_t with unit-variance structural shocks and additive residual noise; VAR lags and dynamic responses are outside the first paper. M0038 added a formula-first Definition 1 in Section 2.

For the robust transformed-cumulant route, additive residual noise is independent of structural shocks and Gaussian, so every linear transform of the noise has zero cumulants above order two. M0038 added this as Assumption 1 in the Section 4 formula sketch.

Scale must be fixed once, not twice. M0034 flags that the manuscript cannot simultaneously use the diagonal-normalized chart diag(B)=1 and also impose unit shock variances when deriving second-order restrictions. M0038 Section 4 now sketches the active route: profile shock variances and residual-noise variances in a diagonal-normalized chart, while keeping pure higher cumulants as the validity backbone.
Population sign-restricted set built from a covariance factor P_* P_*' = B0 B0' + V and restrictions R(P_* Q) >= 0. M0038 added this as Definition 2 in Section 2.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: If V is nonzero, the standard sign-restricted population set is based on B0 B0' + V and generally differs from the no-noise economic sign set. M0038 added a cautious Proposition 1 statement in Section 2.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: B0(D^2 - I)B0' = V, equivalently D^2 - I = B0^{-1} V B0^{-1'}, showing why positive column rescaling generally cannot absorb additive residual noise. M0038 inserted the equation with label eq:column-rescaling-obstruction in Section 2.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: M27 common-chart reported standard-DW set: sign-admissible normalized candidates whose standardized recovered shocks pass a J-test stack containing the no-noise covariance moment and finite higher-moment independence restrictions. M0038 added the Section 3 formula sketch for the covariance-whitened recovered shocks and J-test inversion.
objects.source: vault/papers/Refining set-identification in VARs through independence.md; manuscript/derivations/dw-robust-comparison-diagnostic.md

objects.description: M25 working result: under residual noise, standard DW recovered shocks are not structural shocks. A rich covariance-whitened J-test inversion is generically empty in population, except for structural-coordinate rescaling cases or finite-moment aliases; finite samples can produce falsely small least-rejected regions around pseudo-candidates. M0038 added a cautious Proposition 2 sketch, still pending the M25 proof audit.
objects.proof_status: M25 working derivation complete; needs audit and early Monte Carlo triage

objects.description: Population and sample J-test inversion for standard covariance-whitened DW candidates: g_infty(Q)'Wg_infty(Q) and T ghat(Q)'What ghat(Q), with asymptotic rejection when the population criterion is bounded away from zero. M0038 inserted the sample inversion in Section 3.
objects.verification_status: M25 working derivation complete; needs early MC and audit before draft theorem wording

objects.description: Robust DW-style sign-restricted candidate set that searches over a normalized impact space and uses mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u rather than imposing no-noise recovered-shock covariance. The M0030 off-diagonal residual covariance anchor is superseded after M0034 because it double-normalizes scale in the diag(B)=1 chart unless unit shock variances are separately imposed. M0038 added Definition 3 and the variance-ratio proposal sketch in Section 4.
objects.verification_status: M24 conditional audit passed for the pure local normalized Gaussian-noise higher-cumulant route. M0030/M37 added and audited a six-moment diagonal-anchor variant, but M0034 supersedes that variant: under diag(B)=1 the off-diagonal covariance is Sigma_u,12=b21*sigma_1^2+b12*sigma_2^2 unless unit shock variances are imposed as an extra scale normalization. M0036 supplies the variance-ratio proposal; M40-M45 must audit, draft, rebuild figures, and rebuild evidence around it.

objects.description: Moment-equation representation of the robust DW stack after M0034: third mixed central moments and fourth mixed cumulants of z(B)=B^{-1}u, with transformed second moments entering only as covariance products inside fourth cumulants. The off-diagonal residual covariance anchor is no longer part of the active robust stack under diag(B)=1. M0038 inserted the five-moment stack and fourth-cumulant formulas in Section 4.
objects.verification_status: M24 algebra review passed for centered third moments and fourth cumulants with covariance-product subtractions. M0034 invalidated the added M0030 diagonal-noise covariance anchor under the active scale normalization, so the active pure stack has five moments and uses chi2_5 only as a pointwise guide pending M39 evidence rebuild.

objects.description: M0035 optional covariance screen: for B=[[1,a],[b,1]] and e(B)=B^{-1}u, diagonal residual noise V=diag(nu_1,nu_2) implies E[e1 e2]=(-b*nu_1-a*nu_2)/(1-ab)^2 at the candidate truth. With 0 <= nu_i <= 0.5, the sample recovered covariance must lie in the induced candidate-specific interval.
objects.verification_status: M0035 figure diagnostic generated; algebra and finite-sample interpretation still need M40 adversarial audit before theorem-level prose or M28/M29 rebuild.

objects.description: M0036 variance-ratio robust DW proposal: for B=[[1,a],[b,1]], sample covariance S, structural-shock variances s_i, and diagonal residual noise variances nu_i, require S11=s1+a^2*s2+nu1, S12=b*s1+a*s2, S22=b^2*s1+s2+nu2, and 0 <= nu_i <= 0.5*s_i. This profiles shock and noise variances in the diagonal-normalized chart rather than imposing unit shock variances. M0038 inserted the equality-plus-inequality screen in Section 4.
objects.verification_status: M0036 figure diagnostic generated; algebra, finite-sample equality-plus-inequality behavior, and interpretation of the 50 percent signal-to-noise bound still need M40 adversarial audit before theorem-level prose or M28/M29 rebuild.

objects.description: Working result: under additive Gaussian residual noise, mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u are not shifted by noise. In the bivariate normalized system, local higher-cumulant identification follows when each structural shock has a nonzero third or fourth cumulant. The M0036 variance-ratio robust DW proposal adds the relative noise-to-shock variance screen as extra identifying information. M0038 added a cautious Proposition 3 sketch; M40 still must audit the screen before theorem-level wording.
objects.proof_status: Pure higher-cumulant local-rank audit passed conditionally in M24. M0030/M37 diagonal-anchor pass judgment is superseded by M0034 scale correction. M0036 variance-ratio screen is now the proposal and needs M40 audit before theorem-level wording.

objects.description: Diagnostic statement to revise after M0034: standard-DW accepted mass outside a valid pure robust-DW set warns that no-noise covariance-target precision may be driven by residual-noise misspecification, while a large pure robust-DW set records the information lost by dropping invalid second-order restrictions.
objects.proof_status: M27 formalization complete for the older comparison metric, but M0034 supersedes the diagonal-anchor robust set used in M29. Recompute metrics after M39.

objects.description: M27 overlap metric bundle for the common normalized chart: accepted shares, symmetric Jaccard overlap, directional containment q_{S|R}=mu(S cap R)/mu(S), and warning share d_{S not subset R}=1-q_{S|R}.
Intuitive sign-set geometry is now covered by the top row of the M0036 relative-noise residual grid, which shows sign/covariance accepted sets moving as residual noise increases.
objects.output_source: Proposal visual spine: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py --robust-mode relative and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid.png. A separate covariance-ellipse panel is optional, not required for the first draft.

objects.description: Residual-noise story figure after M0036. The variance-ratio robust row uses pure higher-cumulant J inversion plus a covariance-decomposition feasibility screen with 0 <= nu_i <= 0.5 Var(epsilon_i); in the high-noise column standard DW rejects true B0 while variance-ratio robust DW contains it with much smaller accepted share than the pure row.
objects.output_source: M0036 relative variant: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py --robust-mode relative, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid_figure.md, and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid.png. M0035 absolute-bound comparison remains manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_bounded_noise_robust_grid.png. M0034 pure variant remains manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_pure_robust_noise_grid.png. The earlier diagonal-anchor output manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_grid.png is superseded for the robust row.

objects.description: Grid-pair story after M0036. Figure 1 now uses the variance-ratio robust DW proposal. Figure 2 must be rebuilt with the same robust row, and Figure 3 must be added as a sample-size grid.
objects.output_source: M0036 relative Figure 1 variant: manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid.png and manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid_figure.md. Future outputs: rebuilt Figure 2 with variance-ratio robust DW and new Figure 3 varying T=500,1000,2000. Existing M0030/M28/M29 outputs are superseded for the robust row and must be rebuilt.

objects.description: Planned Figure 3: a sample-size grid with T=500, T=1000, and T=2000, holding the Figure 1 structural non-Gaussianity calibration and Figure 2 residual-noise calibration fixed. It should show finite-sample tightening or persistence of the variance-ratio robust DW set.

Monte Carlo summary must be rebuilt after M0034 because the M29 robust row used the superseded diagonal-anchor statistic. The revised table should compare standard DW with the pure higher-cumulant robust set and the M0036 variance-ratio robust DW proposal.
objects.output_source: Superseded M29 output: manuscript/simulations/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.py, manuscript/simulations/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.md, manuscript/simulations/output/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.json. Rebuild under future task M45.

objects.description: M24 adversarial review of the robust DW higher-cumulant derivation. Conditional pass: cumulant-to-moment algebra, second-moment exclusion, normalization caveat, local rank, Gaussian-noise condition, and bias language are recorded. M37 later audited the post-M0030 diagonal-noise covariance anchor as a separate six-moment estimator gate.

Direct post-M0030 audit of the reported diagonal-noise robust DW estimator. Superseded by M0034: the off-diagonal covariance anchor is not valid in the diag(B)=1 chart unless unit shock variances are also imposed. The pure higher-cumulant part remains the active valid route under independent Gaussian residual noise.

Adversarial review of simulation design and interpretation for noisy sign-set bias, standard DW false sharpening, and robust DW comparison. M30 completed the first audit layer for the M35 triage script: conditional pass for exploratory use only; population-grid and final Monte Carlo audits remain.

Refreshed M28 validation gate for the M0030 revised grid pair. Exact population moment diagnostics support robust-DW truth zeros under diagonal Gaussian residual noise, standard-DW divergence under noisy covariance targets, and weak/Gaussian higher-moment widening toward the covariance anchor. Repeated finite-sample seeds support the high-noise visual story at pointwise chi-square cutoffs, while M29 supplies calibrated evidence.

open_audits.question: Does the M25 standard-DW J-test derivation survive audit, especially the rich-stack generic emptying claim, structural-rescaling exception, and finite-moment alias caveats?

Does the M0036 relative-noise covariance screen correctly profile shock and noise variances in the diag(B)=1 chart, and is the 50 percent noise-to-shock variance bound defensible as an identifying restriction?
